uses: Petranthe verna, also known as the sweet woodruff, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Petranthe verna, also known as Petranthe or Vernal Petranthe, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, commonly found in the Eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Petranthe verna include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
